rationale: The cited paper is used as empirical precedent in the citing paper's hypothesis development, specifically in Table 2 for the expected positive effect of customer size on share of customer wallet. This gives the citation a clear functional role in setting model expectations, not just a generic background list. Although the use is narrow (one linkage rather than the whole framework), it materially informs the conceptual setup of the analysis.
relation_description: The 2004 paper draws on Bowman and Narayandas (2001) to ground its prediction that larger customers concentrate purchases more intensely with vendors, affecting the satisfaction-to-share pathway. Thus, the cited study serves as prior empirical grounding for part of the citing model.
